rubric_items.description: ① Create and test the function that accepts an integer array.
② The function then calculates and returns the average of all the values that EXCEED the running average of their previous numbers.
②ⓑ If a value doesn’t have any previous values, that value will not be considered in the average.
②ⓒ The running average of all the previous numbers is calculated by taking the sum divided by the count starting from the first element till the previous element.
③ If the function cannot determine any such values, it returns zero.
❶ Create and test the method that accepts an array.
❷ The method creates and returns a new array newArray that essentially has a length of array.Length*(array.Length + 1)/2. ❸  First, copy all the values from the original array into the newArray.
❹ Within the nested loop, insert all the previously inserted values,
❹  excluding one of their smallest values, into the newArray.

❺ Iterate through Step 4 until there are no more elements to insert.  ❻ For maximum credit, your time complexity should be O(n²). The space complexity should be O(n²) for the result array. Do not create an additional array. Do not sort the array.

We will have a makeup Test for Problem B on nested loops during the first half of the class on April 10th. You are expected to complete the code within 90 minutes, not 2 or 3 hours!! So, please practice the old HW problems on nested loops. Your makeup Test 2 Problem B will replace the existing score from Problem B (50% of your overall Test 2 score) if it's higher than your existing Problem B score.
